fact: The Silver Bridge stood for about forty years before its collapse (built 1928, collapsed 1967)
The bridge used eyebar chains rather than wire cables, with each link being a single solid piece of steel and no redundancy
source: National Transportation Safety Board, Collapse of U.S. 35 Highway Bridge, Point Pleasant, West Virginia, December 15, 1967 (Report NTSB-HAR-71-1, 1970)

fact: The failure originated from a small corrosion-stress crack approximately 2.5 mm (0.1 in) deep in a single eyebar link
source: National Transportation Safety Board, Collapse of U.S. 35 Highway Bridge, Point Pleasant, West Virginia, December 15, 1967 (Report NTSB-HAR-71-1, 1970)

fact: The bridge collapsed during rush-hour traffic on December 15, 1967
Forty-six people died in the collapse
source: National Transportation Safety Board, Collapse of U.S. 35 Highway Bridge (NTSB-HAR-71-1, 1970); also widely documented in West Virginia state historical records

fact: The eyebar chain design had no second strand (no redundancy), meaning a single link failure caused total collapse
source: National Transportation Safety Board, Collapse of U.S. 35 Highway Bridge (NTSB-HAR-71-1, 1970)
